title: Podman on OSX uses bridged network for rootless containers
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman on OSX seems to rely on bridged network (rootful) although container is configured to be rootless and should use slirp4netns by default.

I deletet the default CNI network labeled "podman" on the remote podman machine and now receive this error when deploying remotely:

`_Error: error configuring network namespace for container 1e283156c1dd890f4ef745160104534b60bfce4c4b06ad4bcc45dcf7172cfc05: CNI network "podman" not found_`

After executing

```
%  podman network create podman
/home/myuser/.config/cni/net.d/podman.conflist

```

Deployment works again.

Command for launching the container is:

`podman -c podman-box run -d --name mynewcontainer -p 5000:5000 myimage:latest bash -c "python3 ./service.py"
`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

`_Error: error configuring network namespace for container 1e283156c1dd890f4ef745160104534b60bfce4c4b06ad4bcc45dcf7172cfc05: CNI network "podman" not found_`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Deployment of rootless container should not rely on default CNI network "podman" (type bridged) but create a slirp4netns network and use that.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Local podman (OSX): podman version 3.4.4
Remote podman (RHEL): podman version 3.4.2

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: We default to bridge networking on OS X as, in versions prior to v4.0, a CNI plugin is required to set up port forwarding from the OS X host to the container in the VM. As such it is strongly not recommended to delete the default network.

Ah ok I see. Perhaps a note in the docs would be good - as the docs state that rootless means slrp4netns by default without further indication. And executing the same command without explicit arguments for network gives different results between OSX and Linux.

Thanks for clarification.
